world.title: Russian strikes kill 16 across Ukraine in worst attack this year
world.description: Russia unleashed its deadliest attack so far this year on the Ukrainian capital Kyiv and other cities overnight, killing 16 people, including a 12-year-old child, and wounding scores, in drone and missile strikes, officials said on Thursday.y.

world.description: Nearly 800 people died and 10,000 were injured as a result of accidents in Northern Ireland in 2023/24, statistics have shown.

business.title: Musk Asks Suppliers to Move at ‘Light Speed’ on Terafab Plan
business.description: Elon Musk’s lieutenants have reached out to chip industry suppliers including Applied Materials Inc., Tokyo Electron Ltd. and Lam Research Corp. for his envisioned Terafab, early steps in an audacious and likely arduous attempt to break into the production of cutting-edge chips.

business.title: Wall Street eyes record, ASX set for flat start
business.description: The Australian sharemarket bucked the trend of rising global markets to close lower on Thursday.

business.title: Stocks to watch, April 16: HDB Financial, HDFC Life, Wipro, NBFCs, Dixon Tech, Delhivery

business.title: Chinabank raises dividends after record 2025 profit
business.description: Chinabank boosts shareholder returns with a higher P7.5-B cash dividend after posting a record P28-B net income in 2025. Learn how its stronger earnings, 4.5% dividend yield, solid ROE and ROA, and Moody’s Baa2 rating reflect the bank’s resilience and confidence in future growth.

business.title: Q1 2026 GDP: China says its economy is accelerating despite Iran war turmoil
business.description: China said its economy expanded by 5.0% in the first quarter compared to the same period last year, exceeding expectations, as the Iran war upends global trade but warned it faced “volatile” external conditions.

business.title: Major UK delivery company plunges into liquidation after angry reviews online
business.description: The business has been focused on providing a emission-free delivery service to help retailers to give same-hour, same-day or next-day delivery to customers, but has received mixed reviews online

science.title: What Happens When Light Goes Boom? Part 1: The Scientist Who Stared at a Glow
science.description: In 1934, a Soviet physicist named Pavel Cherenkov shone gamma rays into a bottle of water and noticed a faint blue glow. So had others before him. They all shrugged and moved on. Cherenkov didn't. What he found — by refusing to dismiss something he didn't understand — turned into one of the most useful phenomena in modern physics.

science.title: Why the Moon Looks Different Across the World
science.description: Differences in perspective can cause the Moon's orientation to change significantly, both across regions and over time.

science.title: Big-Nosed Herbivorous Dinosaur May Have Been Picky Eater
science.description: New research shows that the large-bodied ornithopod dinosaur Muttaburrasaurus langdoni from the mid-Cretaceous of Australia was no ordinary herbivore.

science.title: NASA Shares Artemis II Moon and Earth Photos Taken With Nikon D5
science.description: NASA shares new Artemis II images captured with Nikon cameras, including the D5 and Z9, showcasing detailed views of the Moon, Earth, and life aboard Orion.

science.title: Spatiotemporal light pulses could secure optical communication by masking data
science.description: Researchers at Ben-Gurion University of the Negev have developed a new approach to secure optical communication that hides information in the physical structure of light, making it difficult for unauthorized parties to intercept or decode. The study addresses a growing challenge: advances in quantum computing are expected to weaken many of today's encryption methods. While most security solutions rely on complex mathematical algorithms, this research adds protection earlier in the process—during the transmission of the signal itself.

science.title: A Quiet Region of Italy Is Hiding a Vast Reservoir of Magma
science.description: Scientists have discovered a vast reservoir of magma bubbling beneath the Tuscan soil that may not yet have erupted.

ai.title: Asian Markets Surge Amid Optimism for Iran Deal and Earnings Boost
ai.description: Asian stocks climbed on Thursday, buoyed by optimism for a resolution to the US-Iran conflict and strong earnings reports. The MSCI Asia-Pacific index rose, with Japan's Nikkei setting a new record. Positive earnings from US banks and anticipated profits in the AI sector further fueled market enthusiasm.

ai.title: Karnataka to host 'Bridge to Bengaluru' in Delhi; delegates from 80 countries to join global tech push
ai.description: Bridge to Bengaluru 2026 will act as a curtain-raiser for the 29th edition of the Bengaluru Tech Summit, scheduled to be held from November 17-19, 2026, at the Bangalore International Exhibition Centre (BIEC), under the theme 'AI & Beyond'.

ai.title: EU warns Meta WhatsApp AI fee breaches antitrust rules, orders rollback
ai.description: The European Commission is moving against Meta over changes to WhatsApp that could sideline rival AI assistants. Regulators say the new access fee risks violating competition rules, signalling a broader crackdown on how Big Tech controls emerging AI ecosystems and whether dominant platforms are unfairly limiting access to competitors.

ai.title: Musk's staff reaches out to suppliers for Terafab project, Bloomberg News reports
ai.description: Elon Musk's staff have reached out to chip industry ​suppliers for his Terafab AI chip complex ‌project with SpaceX and Tesla , Bloomberg News reported on Wednesday.

ai.title: Elon Musk Ventures into Chip Industry for Terafab AI Project
ai.description: Elon Musk's staff are engaging with major chip industry suppliers like Applied Materials and Tokyo Electron for his ambitious Terafab AI chip complex, affiliated with SpaceX and Tesla. This move signifies Musk's expansion into semiconductor manufacturing, though the report by Bloomberg News remains unverified by Reuters.
